title: Temporary Draft: Data Dependency Structure
text:
Core Data Inputs







Data asset
Role in pipeline




Psychometric bridge source data
Bridge source for SSQ/PHQ harmonization.


SSQ-only cohort response data
Input scored with IRT-based calibration to align SSQ responses with PHQ-based depression reference logic.


Childhood exposure longitudinal data
Source input for SDoH trajectory derivation in 0-5 and 6-12 year windows.


Integrated secondary analysis dataset
Final integrated dataset joining calibrated depression outcomes and required covariates/exposures for secondary data analysis.

title: Temporary Draft: Data Dependency Structure
Temporary Draft: Data Dependency Structure
text:
Dependency Notes

Psychometric bridge source data informs the calibration parameters.
SSQ-only cohort response data is transformed using the calibration to make depression logic comparable.
Childhood exposure longitudinal data contributes trajectory-derived social determinant features.
Integrated secondary analysis dataset is the endpoint for CO-LUMINATE secondary analyses.

Counts in boxes are distinct participants (USUBJID) and are calculated at render time from available source objects. Integrated dataset count is restricted to participants with both SDoH trajectory coverage and a non-missing depression endpoint. Color mapping links workflow intent to project framing:

Violet nodes: proposal objectives.
Blue nodes: psychometric calibration stage.
Orange nodes: SDoH trajectory stage.
Green nodes: final linkage and integrated dataset stage.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
This Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) specifies the inferential framework for CO-LUMINATE, a longitudinal linked-data study designed to evaluate how social determinants of health influence depression among young people in rural South Africa. The document is written to align with the approved CO-LUMINATE proposal and to govern reproducible analysis of the linked cohort-household platform developed for this project.
The scientific motivation is to estimate both direct and indirect pathways between social stress exposures and depression, with explicit attention to life-course timing, mediation mechanisms, and uncertainty quantification. The analytic strategy is therefore structured around longitudinal exposure characterization, harmonized depression endpoint construction, and causal mediation modeling under prespecified assumptions.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
This Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) specifies the inferential framework for CO-LUMINATE, a longitudinal linked-data study designed to evaluate how social determinants of health influence depression among young people in rural South Africa. The document is written to align with the approved CO-LUMINATE proposal and to govern reproducible analysis of the linked cohort-household platform developed for this project.
The scientific motivation is to estimate both direct and indirect pathways between social stress exposures and depression, with explicit attention to life-course timing, mediation mechanisms, and uncertainty quantification. The analytic strategy is therefore structured around longitudinal exposure characterization, harmonized depression endpoint construction, and causal mediation modeling under prespecified assumptions.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
section: 2. Study Aim, Objectives, And Research Questions
text:
2. Study Aim, Objectives, And Research Questions
The overarching aim is to quantify the longitudinal relationship between social determinants and depression symptoms among young people aged 13-24 years, and to evaluate mediation pathways through behavioral and psychosocial factors. In operational terms, this entails estimating how early and mid-childhood exposure patterns are associated with later depression risk, and whether those associations are explained in part by modifiable mediating factors measured during adolescence and young adulthood.
The proposal-level objectives are implemented in five linked analytical domains. First, the study describes population-level trajectories of socioeconomic position and caregiver co-residency. Second, it characterizes interactions between these social determinants and mediator domains relevant to youth mental health. Third, it defines and summarizes depression prevalence using a harmonized endpoint. Fourth, it estimates direct and mediated effects of social determinants on depression. Fifth, it integrates co-creation principles through interpretation and contextual framing of findings. Food insecurity was part of the original conceptual plan but is treated as unavailable for primary longitudinal trajectory modeling in the finalized linked analysis dataset (see amendment note in Section 12).

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
3. Data Sources And Analytical Cohort
The analytical platform integrates individual-level mental health data from youth cohorts nested within the AHRI HDSS with household-level longitudinal exposure histories from the surveillance system. The linked cohort includes participants from DREAMS, Multilevel, Isisekelo Sempilo, Treatment as Prevention (TasP), and Thetha Nami, with harmonized follow-up structures sufficient for trajectory and mediation analyses.
The primary analysis population includes participants who satisfy both endpoint and linkage requirements: availability of a valid depression endpoint (DPBN) and valid linkage to longitudinal household social-determinant data. Final inferential models are estimated only on participants meeting these criteria. Cohort assembly, exclusions, and denominator transitions are reported transparently in metadata and CONSORT-style flow summaries.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
4. Conceptual Causal Model
The core causal structure is represented by a directed acyclic graph in which childhood social stress exposures affect depression through both direct pathways and indirect pathways operating via behavioral or social mediators. The framework assumes temporally ordered pathways from childhood exposure windows (0-5 years; 6-12 years), through adolescent mediator profiles, to depression outcome status in adolescence and young adulthood.



DAG: Conceptual mediation framework for CO-LUMINATE

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
5. Endpoints, Exposures, Mediators, And Covariates

5.1 Primary Endpoint
The primary endpoint is binary depression status (DPBN). This endpoint is defined on a harmonized psychometric scale that combines directly observed PHQ-based information with SSQ-based calibrated scoring where PHQ is unavailable. Harmonization procedures are fixed prior to mediation model fitting to avoid post-hoc endpoint drift.


5.2 Social Determinant Exposures
Exposure domains are modeled longitudinally and include caregiver co-residency and household socioeconomic conditions. Exposure histories are summarized through latent trajectory/class structures within prespecified childhood windows (0-5 years and 6-12 years), and these classes are encoded as analytical indicators for downstream structural models. The planned household food insecurity trajectory domain is not included in primary analyses because the required longitudinal measure was not obtained in the final analytical data assembly.


5.3 Mediators
The prespecified observed mediator set for primary modeling comprises alcohol use (DNKA), sexual behavior (ESXC), government grant access (GOVG), violence exposure (VLNC), social support (SCSP), and school attendance (SCHL). In addition to this observed parallel mediator representation, a latent mediator phenotype class is estimated as an alternative mediation structure to assess whether mediator clustering yields a more coherent mechanistic profile.


5.4 Confounder Strategy
Two confounder blocks are prespecified. Baseline confounders capture pre-exposure structural context, including maternal and paternal demographic, educational, HIV, partnership, and migration attributes. Mediator-outcome confounders include sex, age, HIV status, orphanhood, external migration, rural residence, and calendar year. Adjustment sets are fixed before primary effect estimation and are applied consistently across model families, with sensitivity analyses evaluating the impact of alternative adjustment scope.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
6. Statistical Principles
All primary inferential analyses adopt a Bayesian estimation framework implemented in Mplus. Effect estimation is based on posterior summaries and 95% credible intervals, with primary interpretation focused on interval compatibility with null and effect magnitude. Statistical significance language is used cautiously and is secondary to estimation and uncertainty reporting.
Primary estimands are: (i) direct effects of childhood exposure class indicators on depression; (ii) specific natural indirect effects through each observed mediator; and (iii) natural indirect effects through the latent mediator phenotype class. These estimands are interpreted under standard causal mediation assumptions, including exchangeability conditional on measured covariates, positivity, and correct model specification.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
7. Analytical Workflow

7.1 Descriptive And Data Quality Analyses
Descriptive analyses quantify cohort composition, missingness structure, prevalence of endpoint and mediator domains, and distribution of exposure classes. These analyses serve both quality control and interpretive context and are reported before causal models.


7.2 Longitudinal Exposure Modeling
Exposure trajectories are modeled separately in childhood windows (0-5 years; 6-12 years). Candidate class structures are evaluated using fit indices, class interpretability, and practical constraints including minimum class prevalence. Selected class solutions are locked prior to mediation estimation.


7.3 Depression Harmonization
Depression harmonization uses calibrated psychometric modeling to map SSQ-only records onto a PHQ-referenced depression scale, after which a fixed binary endpoint (DPBN) is derived. This harmonization stage is treated as upstream data preparation and not iteratively tuned based on mediation outcomes.


7.4 Primary Causal Mediation Models
Two model families are estimated to address complementary mechanistic assumptions. The first uses observed parallel mediators, where each mediator is regressed on exposure indicators and confounders, and the depression endpoint is regressed on all mediators, exposures, and confounders. Natural indirect effects are estimated via parametric g-computation syntax in Mplus (MODEL INDIRECT ... (CAUSAL)), pooled over multiple imputation/plausible-value draws.
The second model family uses a latent categorical mediator phenotype, where exposure indicators predict latent class membership and the endpoint is modeled as a function of latent mediator class, exposure indicators, and confounders. This approach evaluates whether mediator co-occurrence patterns provide stronger explanatory structure than separate observed mediators.
For pooled Bayesian analyses, the target imputation/plausible-value framework is m = 100 draws. Posterior estimates are extracted and summarized in publication-ready effect tables with explicit interval reporting.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
8. Missing Data And Imputation
Missing data are handled through a structured imputation-aware modeling workflow integrated with Bayesian estimation. Covariate and mediator missingness are addressed under model-based assumptions consistent with the imputation design. Participants with structurally missing core linkage components or unavailable primary endpoint are excluded according to prespecified rules. Sensitivity comparisons include reduced models and complete-case contrasts to assess robustness of substantive conclusions.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
9. Sensitivity, Robustness, And Secondary Analyses
Robustness is assessed along four dimensions: mediator representation (observed vs latent), class solution alternatives, covariate adjustment sets, and depression endpoint threshold specification. Additional sensitivity summaries include effect-stability diagnostics and E-value style reporting for key indirect pathways where applicable. These analyses are interpreted as robustness evidence and do not replace the prespecified primary estimands.
Subgroup summaries by sex, age band, rural context, and HIV status are reported descriptively or as exploratory heterogeneity assessments unless explicitly prespecified for confirmatory inference.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
10. Reporting Standards
All primary and sensitivity outputs are reported with clear separation between estimand definition, model specification, and interpretation. Tables and figures present posterior estimates, interval bounds, and pathway labels mapped to the conceptual framework. Interpretation emphasizes direction, magnitude, and uncertainty, and avoids causal overstatement beyond assumptions supported by design and measured covariates.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
section: 11. Reproducibility, Governance, And Data Use Constraints
text:
11. Reproducibility, Governance, And Data Use Constraints
The SAP is operationalized through version-controlled scripts organized into data preparation, model fitting, extraction, and reporting modules. Analytical objects (.rds), tables, and figures are generated from scripted pipelines and traced to model configuration files.
Participant-level private data remain in controlled environments. Public-facing website outputs are restricted to non-disclosive summaries, derived figures, and metadata artifacts. Access to full internal proposal and restricted analysis products remains governed by the Principal Investigator and project governance procedures.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
text:
12. SAP Versioning And Amendments
This SAP reflects the current CO-LUMINATE scientific plan and linked-data workflow. Any amendments to endpoint definition, exposure class selection, covariate sets, mediator structure, or inferential model family will be logged as SAP updates with explicit rationale, implementation date, and impact statement on prior results.

12.1 Amendment Record
On March 5, 2026, the SAP was amended to document that food insecurity was not obtained in the finalized longitudinal exposure build for this phase of CO-LUMINATE. As a result, food insecurity is removed from the primary longitudinal exposure specification and from the primary observed mediator set used for confirmatory mediation models in this SAP version.

title: Proposal
text: CO-LUMINATE investigates how social stress exposures shape depressive symptoms among young people in rural South Africa over time. The work links cohort mental health outcomes with AHRI HDSS household trajectories to support longitudinal and mediation-focused analysis.

title: Proposal
text: CO-LUMINATE investigates how social stress exposures shape depressive symptoms among young people in rural South Africa over time. The work links cohort mental health outcomes with AHRI HDSS household trajectories to support longitudinal and mediation-focused analysis.

title: Proposal
text:
Scope

Population focus: adolescents and young adults aged 13-24 years.
Data structure: linked longitudinal cohort and HDSS records.
Core domains: socioeconomic status, food insecurity, caregiver co-residency, and related social stress exposures.
Outcome focus: symptoms of depression measured repeatedly over time.
Co-creation: youth partners contribute to interpretation and relevance framing.

title: Proposal
text:
What The Project Will Achieve

Build and maintain a longitudinal linked dataset for youth mental health and social determinants.
Quantify direct and mediation relationships between key social exposures and depression outcomes.
Characterize exposure patterns and risk trajectories across cohorts and time.
Generate actionable evidence for policy, program design, and future research in LMIC settings.

title: Proposal
text:
Planned Deliverables

Curated linked analysis datasets with supporting metadata and documentation.
Reproducible statistical workflows, technical reports, and presentation outputs.
Evidence summaries for research, policy, and stakeholder engagement.
Co-created interpretation products to strengthen contextual validity and uptake.

title: Proposal
text:
Access To Full Proposal
This page presents a high-level summary for website readers.
The full proposal is an internal document and is not published on the website.
Requests for the full proposal can be directed to the study team and will be considered at the Principal Investigator’s discretion.

title: Metadata
text:
Study Design Extract
The CO-LUMINATE linked dataset connects individual-level mental health outcomes from cohort studies to household-level longitudinal HDSS records. The objective is to estimate longitudinal direct and mediation relationships between social determinants and depressive symptoms among youth aged 13-24.
The analytical design combines:

HDSS longitudinal household exposure histories.
Cohort-based depression outcomes measured with SSQ-14 and PHQ-9.
Linkage across five cohorts nested within AHRI HDSS.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
This report reflects the detailed structure of the psychometric source index, including:

Introduction and clinical rationale.
Methods (setting, sampling, measures, and statistical workflow).
Results (structure, invariance, calibration, and harmonization).
Interpretation for transportable depression-outcome derivation.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
This report reflects the detailed structure of the psychometric source index, including:

Introduction and clinical rationale.
Methods (setting, sampling, measures, and statistical workflow).
Results (structure, invariance, calibration, and harmonization).
Interpretation for transportable depression-outcome derivation.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
Introduction
The primary aim is to derive a rigorous and evidence-based SSQ threshold aligned to PHQ-9 probable depression (PHQ-9 >= 10) for adolescents and young adults. The source analysis compares SSQ-14, a theoretically refined SSQ-10 subset, and reference PHQ-9 structure to support harmonized depression classification in CO-LUMINATE linked analyses.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
Methods

Setting and Study Design
The work is embedded in the Isisekelo Sempilo trial context in rural uMkhanyakude (KwaZulu-Natal), using AHRI surveillance-linked data structures and clinically anchored psychometric evaluation.


Participants and Sampling
The psychometric analysis cohort in the source index is restricted to participants with concurrent baseline SSQ-14 and PHQ-9 information, focused on adolescents and young adults.


Measures and Construct Strategy










Instrument
Role
Notes




PHQ-9
Reference depression benchmark and calibration anchor.
Polytomous DSM-aligned depressive symptom scale.


SSQ-14
Original culturally grounded distress measure.
Binary symptom measure with broader CMD content.


SSQ-10 (theoretical subset)
Depression-focused subset for harmonized screening and transportability.
Retains core depressive/cognitive-affective items and excludes less specific items.







Statistical Workflow from Source Index

Descriptive profiling and item-level diagnostics.
Monte Carlo split-sample EFA for factor-retention stability.
Monte Carlo CFA for fit verification of candidate solutions.
Reliability and measurement invariance checks across sex and age groups.
IRT-based harmonization and ROC operating-point comparison.
Threshold translation and transportability checks for SSQ-only scoring.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
Core Concepts









Concept
Meaning




Latent structure
Underlying factor organization captured by candidate psychometric models.


Measurement invariance
Stability of measurement properties across demographic and clinical strata.


Calibration
Alignment of score scales or thresholds between SSQ and PHQ.


Discrimination
Ability to separate probable depression from non-cases.


Transportability
Ability to apply harmonized scoring rules in cohorts lacking PHQ.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
Results Summary

Participant and Baseline Signal Summary
Key source-index highlights:

Analysis sample reported in source results: N = 843.
PHQ-defined probable depression prevalence in source results: approximately 4.0%.
Several SSQ items show stronger alignment with PHQ severity than excluded SSQ items, supporting subset refinement logic.



Structural Validity and Stability
Source-index interpretation indicates:

PHQ-9 shows strong practical support for unidimensional scoring despite methodological divergence in retention heuristics.
SSQ-14 shows multidimensional tendencies with distinct clusters, motivating subset refinement.
SSQ-10 resolves toward stable and interpretable core dimensions after artifact inspection (singleton-factor handling).




Factor Retention and Structure





Primary Psychometric Results Overview




Invariance and Reliability
Source findings support robust CFI-based invariance behavior across sex and age group partitions for key models, with RMSEA behavior interpreted in context of subgroup sensitivity and model complexity. Reliability indicators remain high enough to support harmonized use in downstream analyses.


Harmonization and Calibration
Source-index harmonization findings emphasize:

ROC-only cutoffs can inflate predicted prevalence relative to PHQ reference prevalence.
IRT-anchored approaches better control false positives and improve transportability to SSQ-only settings.
Operating-point choice should prioritize intended downstream use (screening sensitivity vs analytic specificity).




IRT and ROC Performance





SSQ-08 Validity Evidence





SSQ-10 Parallel Analysis Heatmap

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
Interpretation

Statistical Interpretation

Harmonization quality depends on jointly evaluating structure, invariance, reliability, and calibration, not any single metric.
The SSQ-10 configuration provides a pragmatic balance between construct coherence and operational usability.
IRT-anchored translation to PHQ-compatible thresholds is preferred where prevalence inflation from naive thresholds is unacceptable.



Operational Interpretation

Harmonized depression derivation supports comparability across linked CO-LUMINATE cohorts.
The framework is suitable for secondary longitudinal analyses where instruments differ by cohort.
Sensitivity analyses around threshold rules remain essential in subsequent objective-level analyses.

title: Psychometric Harmonization Report
text:
Reporting Note
This website report is a communication-focused detailed summary aligned to the source index narrative. Full reproducible technical outputs remain in the dedicated psychometric workstream.

title: CO-LUMINATE Psychometric Harmonization
text:
Scope

Objective: establish comparability between SSQ and PHQ depression measures in the CO-LUMINATE psychometric stream.
Outputs summarize structure stability, calibration performance, and score harmonization evidence.

title: CO-LUMINATE Psychometric Harmonization
text:
Analytical Workflow

Monte Carlo EFA for factor-structure stability.
Monte Carlo CFA for model-fit and invariance evaluation.
IRT plus ROC calibration for harmonized outcome interpretation.
Integration of diagnostics into analysis-ready guidance.

text:
Implications for CO-LUMINATE

Preliminary evidence supports harmonized use of SSQ and PHQ constructs for depression-outcome derivation.
Psychometric comparability enables more coherent downstream modeling across linked datasets.
Next step: consolidate finalized psychometric outputs into the integrated project analysis pipeline.

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Project Overview

CO-LUMINATE links individual mental health outcomes from cohort studies to AHRI HDSS household trajectories.
Main focus is longitudinal relationships between social determinants and depressive symptoms in youth aged 13-24.
Linked sample size: 5,279 adolescents and young people.

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Study Question

How do social stress exposures relate to depressed mood through time?
Priority social determinants:

Socioeconomic status (SES)
Caregiver co-residency and parental absence
Household composition and crowding

Outcomes use rigorous depression measures from cohort studies (SSQ-14 and PHQ-9).

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Data Linkage Design

Start from individual-level cohort participants nested in AHRI HDSS.
Link each participant to longitudinal household records across multiple waves.
Derive aligned exposure windows for:

Early childhood (0-5)
Middle childhood (6-12)
Entire childhood (0-12)

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Data Sources (Cohorts)

DREAMS
Multilevel
Isisekelo Sempilo
Treatment as Prevention (TasP)
Thetha Nami

These cohorts provide depression outcomes that are not routinely available in HDSS-only data.

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Dataset Outputs

Harmonized participant-level analysis datasets for mental health and social exposures.
Derived household social stress variables and documentation/codebook-ready metadata.
Reproducible preparation pipeline for downstream statistical analysis objectives.

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Novel Contribution

Integrates rigorous depression outcomes with long-run HDSS social exposure histories.
Enables testing of direct and mediation pathways between social determinants and youth depression.
Supports context-grounded interpretation in a low- and middle-income country setting.

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Co-Creation Component

The project includes 11 Youth Co-Creators (YCC).
YCC input is used to refine interpretation, framing, and relevance of analyses.
Co-creation and secondary data analysis are integrated into one evidence-generation process.

title: CO-LUMINATE Dataset Derivation, Documentation and Codebook
text:
Thank You

CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)

title: About CO-LUMINATE
text:
CO-LUMINATE: Co-Creating Knowledge On The Longitudinal Direct And Mediation Relationships Between Youth Mental Health And Social Stress Exposures
This project investigates the longitudinal relationship between social determinants (SES, and caregiver co-residency) and depression symptoms among South African youth aged 13-24.
Leveraging the Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI) Health and Demographic Surveillance System (HDSS), the study creates the CO-LUMINATE dataset by linking longitudinal data from 5,279 adolescents.
The project employs a co-creation model that engages 11 Youth Co-Creators (YCC) to provide thought leadership that refines and nuances the proposed research.
Through co-creation and secondary data analysis, the project generates transformative insights into how key social determinants of mental health relate to symptoms of depression among South African young people.

title: About CO-LUMINATE
text:
CO-LUMINATE: Co-Creating Knowledge On The Longitudinal Direct And Mediation Relationships Between Youth Mental Health And Social Stress Exposures
This project investigates the longitudinal relationship between social determinants (SES, and caregiver co-residency) and depression symptoms among South African youth aged 13-24.
Leveraging the Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI) Health and Demographic Surveillance System (HDSS), the study creates the CO-LUMINATE dataset by linking longitudinal data from 5,279 adolescents.
The project employs a co-creation model that engages 11 Youth Co-Creators (YCC) to provide thought leadership that refines and nuances the proposed research.
Through co-creation and secondary data analysis, the project generates transformative insights into how key social determinants of mental health relate to symptoms of depression among South African young people.

title: About CO-LUMINATE
text:
Novel Contribution

The project integrates rigorous longitudinal data linkage with a formal youth co-creation process rather than treating youth input as consultative only.
It focuses on direct and mediation pathways between social stress exposures and depression symptoms in a population-level LMIC youth sample.
It produces actionable, contextually grounded evidence for advancing youth mental health research and response in South Africa.

title: CO-LUMINATE: Co-Creating Knowledge On The Longitudinal Direct And Mediation Relationships Between Youth Mental Health And Social Stress Exposures
text:
Youth Mental Health and Social Stress Exposures




Co-Creating Knowledge On The Longitudinal Direct And Mediation Relationships Between Youth Mental Health And Social Stress Exposures







This project investigates the longitudinal relationship between social determinants and depression symptoms among South African youth aged 13-24, combining rigorous secondary data analysis with youth-led co-creation.

title: CO-LUMINATE: Co-Creating Knowledge On The Longitudinal Direct And Mediation Relationships Between Youth Mental Health And Social Stress Exposures
text:
Project Overview
CO-LUMINATE leverages the Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI) Health and Demographic Surveillance System (HDSS) to create a longitudinal linked dataset of 5,279 adolescents and young people. The dataset supports analysis of how social determinants of mental health, including socioeconomic status (SES) and caregiver co-residency, relate to depressive symptoms over time.
The project also uses a structured co-creation model with 11 Youth Co-Creators (YCC), who contribute meaningful thought leadership to refine, guide, and nuance the research process and interpretation.

title: CO-LUMINATE: Co-Creating Knowledge On The Longitudinal Direct And Mediation Relationships Between Youth Mental Health And Social Stress Exposures
text:
Study Design



1



Longitudinal Linkage


Individual-level depression outcomes are linked to household HDSS trajectories to assess direct and mediation relationships through time.








2



Secondary Data Analysis


Longitudinal analyses evaluate relationships between social stress exposures (including SES and caregiver co-residency) and symptoms of depression.








3



Youth Co-Creation


Eleven YCC members provide co-created input to strengthen relevance, interpretation, and context-sensitive framing of findings.

title: CO-LUMINATE Mediation to Outcome Preliminary Analysis
text:
Analysis Scope

Objective: assess associations between potential mediators and depression outcome (DPBN).
Unadjusted model: each mediator fitted independently (bivariate association).
Base model: each mediator fitted separately, adjusted for Sex, Age, HIV, visit year, migration, rural residence, and orphanhood.
Fully adjusted model: all candidate mediators entered simultaneously with the same covariates.
Data source used at build time: private ADaM dt_multistudies (no row-level data saved in project objects).

title: CO-LUMINATE Mediation to Outcome Preliminary Analysis
CO-LUMINATE Mediation to Outcome Preliminary Analysis
text: Forest + Pattern Evidence

title: CO-LUMINATE Mediation to Outcome Preliminary Analysis
text:
Interpretation


- Fully adjusted associations indicate the strongest independent mediator candidates are: Experienced Violence (aOR 2.11 (1.68, 2.67)); Food Insecure (aOR 1.81 (1.44, 2.26)); Ever Had Sex (aOR 1.61 (1.20, 2.17)); No Government Grant (aOR 1.36 (1.06, 1.75))

- Non-significant in the fully adjusted model: Drank Alcohol, In School, Has Social Support . These results support prioritizing robust signals in mediation pathway testing while retaining weaker variables as supporting covariates where scientifically justified.

title: CO-LUMINATE Mediation to Outcome Preliminary Analysis
text:
Object Governance

Script used: PRJ-CO-LUMINATE/_tools/_scripts/build_mediation_prelim_objects.R
Saved objects: aggregated outcome-association summaries, inter-association/correlation summaries, pattern summaries, and presentation-ready figure.
Explicitly excluded from _tools/_objects: participant-level datasets and row-level extracts.
Build rows analyzed: 6428 participants.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
Motivation

CO-LUMINATE links harmonized youth mental-health data to AHRI HDSS social-exposure histories.
Linked analysis cohort: 5,279 adolescents and young people aged 13-24 years.
SAP focus: quantify longitudinal social-determinant effects on depression and mediation through behavioral pathways.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
SAP-Aligned Aim and Questions

Aim: assess the longitudinal impact of social determinants on youth depression using a causal mediation framework.
Research question 4.1: how are social determinants associated longitudinally with depression among young people?
Research question 4.2: what are the causal pathways between low SES, crowding, caregiver co-residency, and depression?
Research question 4.3: are these relationships mediated by migration, substance use, and sexual behavior?

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
Developmental Cascade Perspective


Time-ordered modeling links life-course household exposures to later behavioral and depression outcomes.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
Causal Mediation Structure
  

Click the DAG to open the full-size SVG in a new tab (browser zoom supported).

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
DAG Components

Exposure domain: low SES, crowding, caregiver co-residency (longitudinal HDSS).
Mediator domain (SAP, individual-level): migration, substance use, and sexual behaviour.
Outcome domain: depression derived from harmonized SSQ-14/PHQ-9 framework.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
SAP Mediators (Explicit)

Migration
Substance use
Sexual behaviour
These mediators are tested as behavioural pathways between social determinants and depression.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
section: Youth Co-Creation Input to the SAP
text:
Youth Co-Creation Input to the SAP

Youth Advisory Group (YAG) develops a lay DAG from photo-diary and narrative sessions.
Co-created DAG is refined with researchers and fed into the causal analysis plan.
YAG interpretation workshop is used to contextualize and ground-truth findings.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
section: SAP Analysis Plan: A to D
text:
SAP Analysis Plan: A to D

A: describe social-determinant trajectories using longitudinal latent class analysis.
B: estimate interactions between determinants and mediators (migration, substance use, sexual behaviour) with mixed-effects regression.
C: evaluate SSQ-14 against PHQ-9, derive age-specific cutoffs (13-19, 20-24), estimate prevalence by wave.
D: fit confounder-adjusted logistic models, then estimate causal effects using DAG-guided g-computation.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
Causal Estimation Strategy (SAP Step D)

Use adjusted logistic models to identify social factors associated with depression.
Include associated social factors as causal exposures in the mediation framework.
Use g-computation to estimate effects under time-varying exposures.
Estimate mediation by behavioral factors at individual level (migration, substance use, sexual behavior).

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
Planned Outputs and Translation

Longitudinal linked dataset and reproducible analytic workflow.
Pathway-specific evidence on direct and mediated social-determinant effects on depression.
Policy-relevant signals for social protection, crowding-responsive housing interventions, and caregiver-support programming.
Scalable method for SAPRIN and APCC network contexts.

title: The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
text:
Thank You

CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)
SAP content source: _assets/proposal/proposal_content.md (Sections 4.2 and 5.2.2).

title: Preliminary Mediator Associations Report
text:
Source Alignment (Dataset Preparation)
The report mirrors the dataset preparation source workflow:

Recode and harmonize analysis variables from dt_multistudies.
Fit three logistic models for mediator-to-outcome association.
Quantify pairwise mediator dependence with OR and Cramer’s V.
Provide interpretation anchored to fully adjusted and pairwise strength patterns.

Fully adjusted model
DPBN ~ DNKA + ESXC + FDSC + GOVG + VLNC + SCHL + SCSP + SEX + AGE + HIVS + VSTY + EXTMG + RURAL (+ ORPH as specified in source build)
Independent mediator signal with all candidate mediators entered simultaneously.

title: Preliminary Mediator Associations Report
text:
Interpretation

Outcome Association Interpretation


- Fully adjusted associations indicate the strongest independent mediator candidates are: Experienced Violence (aOR 2.11 (1.68, 2.67)); Food Insecure (aOR 1.81 (1.44, 2.26)); Ever Had Sex (aOR 1.61 (1.20, 2.17)); No Government Grant (aOR 1.36 (1.06, 1.75))
- Non-significant in the fully adjusted model: Drank Alcohol, In School, Has Social Support . These results support prioritizing robust signals in mediation pathway testing while retaining weaker variables as supporting covariates where scientifically justified.


- Unadjusted estimates are descriptive; fully adjusted estimates should guide prioritization.




Pairwise Interpretation


- Ever Had Sex vs In School: OR 0.08, Cramer's V 0.50.
- Drank Alcohol vs Ever Had Sex: OR 2.85, Cramer's V 0.25.
- Ever Had Sex vs No Government Grant: OR 2.68, Cramer's V 0.24.


- Inter-association identifies co-patterning among mediators and does not imply causal direction.

title: Preliminary Mediator Associations Report
text:
Correlation Reporting Decision
Correlation heatmap and rho columns are intentionally omitted in this report version.
For binary 2x2 mediator pairs, the correlation measure (phi/rho in this coding) is numerically equivalent to Cramer’s V; therefore, Cramer’s V is retained as the single strength metric.

title: Longitudinal Linkage
text: This page documents the longitudinal linkage stage that combines mental health outcome harmonization and SDoH trajectory information into the final integrated secondary analysis dataset.

title: Longitudinal Linkage
text: This page documents the longitudinal linkage stage that combines mental health outcome harmonization and SDoH trajectory information into the final integrated secondary analysis dataset.

title: Longitudinal Linkage
text:
Goal Alignment
The linkage workflow directly supports the project objectives:

Build and maintain a linked analysis dataset.
Quantify direct and mediation relationships for depression outcomes.
Characterize social determinant trajectories across childhood windows.

title: Longitudinal Linkage
Longitudinal Linkage
text:
Interpretation

Stage 1 calibrates depression information for SSQ-only records.
Stage 2 derives SDoH trajectory coverage across 0-5 and 6-12 windows.
Stage 3 integrates both streams and keeps participants with both: Depression endpoint and SDoH trajectory coverage.

title: Longitudinal Linkage
text:
Completion Criteria
This stage is complete when:

Depression endpoint values are available for integrated mental health records.
SDoH trajectory coverage is available across childhood windows.
Final integrated data retains only participants meeting both criteria.

title: Longitudinal Linkage
text:
Notes

Counts are distinct participants (USUBJID) and are calculated at render time.
Violet nodes: proposal objectives.
Blue nodes: psychometric stage.
Orange nodes: SDoH trajectory stage.
Green nodes: final integration stage.

title: Presentations
text:
Open presentation in a new tab




This presentation summarizes the core workflow used to derive and document the CO-LUMINATE longitudinal linked dataset, including linkage design, cohort inputs, and analysis-ready outputs.







Open presentation in a new tab




This presentation reports latent class growth and joint trajectory findings across early childhood (0-5), middle childhood (6-12), and entire childhood (0-12), and maps trajectory patterns to adolescent and young adult depression outcomes.







Open presentation in a new tab




This presentation summarizes the preliminary psychometric work on SSQ-PHQ comparability, including factor-structure stability, calibration performance, and harmonized depression-outcome interpretation.







Open presentation in a new tab




This presentation summarizes preliminary association analyses between potential mediators and depression outcome, including the base vs fully adjusted models, integrated forest-plot evidence, pattern-based support plots, and interpretation for mediation-model prioritization.







Open presentation in a new tab




This presentation introduces a longitudinal causal mediation framework that links childhood social-determinant trajectories to adolescent depression through behavioral phenotypes, with emphasis on pathway decomposition and intervention-relevant interpretation.

title: Reports
text: This section hosts project reports, technical summaries, and analysis outputs.

title: Reports
text: This section hosts project reports, technical summaries, and analysis outputs.

title: Reports
text:
Available Reports

Psychometric Harmonization

Open Report
Abstract: This report documents the psychometric harmonization workflow used to align SSQ and PHQ depression constructs, including structural validation, invariance checks, calibration diagnostics, and practical threshold interpretation for transportable scoring.
Keywords: psychometric harmonization; SSQ; PHQ-9; measurement invariance; factor structure; calibration; IRT
Coverage: detailed source-aligned psychometric introduction/methods/results narrative, psychometric concepts, harmonization workflow, structural diagnostics, calibration evidence, and interpretation for SSQ-PHQ comparability.
Visual assets: psychometric figure library in _assets.



Preliminary Mediator Associations

Open Report
Abstract: This report summarizes preliminary associations between candidate mediators and depression outcome, presenting unadjusted, base-adjusted, and fully adjusted models alongside pairwise mediator dependence metrics to guide mediation-model prioritization.
Keywords: mediator associations; depression outcome; logistic regression; adjusted odds ratios; Cramer’s V; pairwise dependence
Coverage: detailed source-aligned dataset preparation methods, model specifications, outcome associations, pairwise inter-association table (OR and Cramer’s V), and interpretation notes.
Source objects: PRJ-CO-LUMINATE/_tools/_objects/mediation_prelim_*
